duas.title: Talbiyah
duas.arabic: لَبَّيْكَ اللَّهُمَّ لَبَّيْكَ، لَبَّيْكَ لَا شَرِيكَ لَكَ لَبَّيْكَ، إِنَّ الْحَمْدَ وَالنِّعْمَةَ لَكَ وَالْمُلْكَ، لَا شَرِيكَ لَكَ
duas.transliteration: Labbayk Allāhumma labbayk. Labbayk lā sharīka laka labbayk. Innal-ḥamda wan-ni'mata laka wal-mulk. Lā sharīka lak.
duas.translation: Here I am, O Allah, here I am. Here I am, You have no partner, here I am. Verily all praise, grace, and sovereignty belong to You. You have no partner.
duas.when_to_recite: From the moment of entering Ihram until throwing the first stone at Jamarat al-Aqabah on the 10th of Dhul Hijjah
duas.notes: Recite loudly — it is Sunnah for men to raise their voices. Women recite quietly.

duas.title: Intention for Hajj
duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أُرِيدُ الْحَجَّ فَيَسِّرْهُ لِي وَتَقَبَّلْهُ مِنِّي
duas.transliteration: Allāhumma innī urīdul-ḥajja fayassirhu lī wa taqabbalhu minnī.
duas.translation: O Allah, I intend to perform Hajj, so make it easy for me and accept it from me.
duas.when_to_recite: Upon putting on the Ihram garments and making the intention
duas.source: Authenticated by scholars of Hajj fiqh
duas.notes: The intention is in the heart; verbal expression is recommended.

duas.title: Du'a at the Start of Tawaf
duas.arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُمَّ إِيمَانًا بِكَ وَتَصْدِيقًا بِكِتَابِكَ وَوَفَاءً بِعَهْدِكَ وَاتِّبَاعًا لِسُنَّةِ نَبِيِّكَ مُحَمَّدٍ ﷺ
duas.transliteration: Bismillāhi wallāhu akbar. Allāhumma īmānan bika wa taṣdīqan bikitābika wa wafā'an bi'ahdika wattibā'an lisunnati nabiyyika Muḥammadin ﷺ.
duas.translation: In the name of Allah and Allah is the Greatest. O Allah, [I begin this tawaf] with faith in You, affirming Your Book, fulfilling Your covenant, and following the Sunnah of Your Prophet Muhammad ﷺ.
duas.when_to_recite: Upon reaching the Black Stone and beginning each circuit of Tawaf
duas.notes: Touch or kiss the Black Stone if possible, otherwise point toward it.

duas.title: Du'a at the Black Stone (Hajar al-Aswad)
duas.arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ
duas.transliteration: Bismillāhi wallāhu akbar.
duas.translation: In the name of Allah, and Allah is the Greatest.
duas.when_to_recite: Each time you pass the Black Stone during Tawaf
duas.notes: If you cannot touch the Black Stone due to crowds, simply point toward it and say this du'a.

duas.title: Du'a at the Yemeni Corner
duas.arabic: رَبَّنَا آتِنَا فِي الدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ حَسَنَةً وَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ
duas.transliteration: Rabbanā ātinā fid-dunyā ḥasanatan wa fil-ākhirati ḥasanatan wa qinā 'adhāban-nār.
duas.translation: Our Lord, give us good in this world and good in the Hereafter, and protect us from the punishment of the Fire.
duas.when_to_recite: Between the Yemeni Corner (Rukn al-Yamani) and the Black Stone during Tawaf
duas.notes: The Prophet ﷺ recited this du'a in every circuit between these two corners.

duas.title: Du'a at Maqam Ibrahim
duas.arabic: وَاتَّخِذُوا مِن مَّقَامِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ مُصَلًّى
duas.transliteration: Wattakhidhū min maqāmi Ibrāhīma muṣallā.
duas.translation: And take the station of Ibrahim as a place of prayer.
duas.when_to_recite: After completing 7 circuits of Tawaf, before praying 2 rakaat at Maqam Ibrahim
duas.notes: Pray 2 rakaat behind Maqam Ibrahim after every Tawaf.

duas.title: Du'a at Safa
duas.arabic: إِنَّ الصَّفَا وَالْمَرْوَةَ مِن شَعَائِرِ اللَّهِ، أَبْدَأُ بِمَا بَدَأَ اللَّهُ بِهِ. اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ. لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
duas.transliteration: Innaṣ-ṣafā wal-marwata min sha'ā'irillāh. Abd'au bimā bada'allāhu bih. Allāhu akbar, Allāhu akbar, Allāhu akbar. Lā ilāha illallāhu waḥdahu lā sharīka lah, lahul-mulku walahul-ḥamdu wahuwa 'alā kulli shay'in qadīr.
duas.translation: Indeed, Safa and Marwa are among the symbols of Allah. I begin with what Allah began with. Allah is the Greatest, Allah is the Greatest, Allah is the Greatest. There is no god but Allah alone, with no partner. To Him belongs sovereignty and praise, and He has power over everything.
duas.when_to_recite: Upon climbing Safa at the beginning of each Sa'i and while facing the Qiblah
duas.notes: Raise your hands in du'a after reciting this and make any supplication you wish.

duas.title: Best Du'a of Arafat
duas.arabic: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
duas.transliteration: Lā ilāha illallāhu waḥdahu lā sharīka lah, lahul-mulku walahul-ḥamdu wahuwa 'alā kulli shay'in qadīr.
duas.translation: There is no god but Allah alone, with no partner. To Him belongs sovereignty and praise, and He has power over everything.
duas.when_to_recite: Throughout the standing at Arafat — repeat abundantly
duas.notes: This is the most virtuous dhikr to repeat at Arafat. Combine it with personal du'a in your own language.

duas.title: Comprehensive Du'a for Arafat
duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّكَ تَسْمَعُ كَلَامِي، وَتَرَى مَكَانِي، وَتَعْلَمُ سِرِّي وَعَلَانِيَتِي، لَا يَخْفَى عَلَيْكَ شَيْءٌ مِنْ أَمْرِي. أَنَا الْبَائِسُ الْفَقِيرُ، الْمُسْتَغِيثُ الْمُسْتَجِيرُ، الْوَجِلُ الْمُشْفِقُ. أَسْأَلُكَ مَسْأَلَةَ الْمِسْكِينِ، وَأَبْتَهِلُ إِلَيْكَ ابْتِهَالَ الْمُذْنِبِ الذَّلِيلِ، وَأَدْعُوكَ دُعَاءَ الْخَائِفِ الضَّرِيرِ
duas.transliteration: Allāhumma innaka tasma'u kalāmī, wa tarā makānī, wa ta'lamu sirrī wa 'alāniyatī, lā yakhfā 'alayka shay'un min amrī. Anal-bā'isul-faqīr, al-mustaghīthul-mustajīr, al-wajilul-mushfiq. As'aluka mas'alatal-miskīn, wa abtahilu ilayka ibtihālal-mudhnibidh-dhalīl, wa ad'ūka du'ā'al-khā'ifidh-dharīr.
duas.translation: O Allah, You hear my words, You see my situation, You know my hidden and open affairs — nothing of my matter is hidden from You. I am wretched and poor, seeking Your help and refuge, fearful and anxious. I ask You with the asking of the destitute, I plead to You with the pleading of the humbled sinner, and I call upon You with the supplication of the fearful afflicted.
duas.when_to_recite: At Arafat during the wuquf
duas.notes: Follow this with personal du'a for yourself, your family, and the ummah.

duas.title: Du'a upon Arriving at Muzdalifah
duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّ هَذِهِ مُزْدَلِفَةُ جُمِعَتْ فِيهَا أَلْسِنَةٌ شَتَّى تَسْأَلُكَ حَوَائِجَ شَتَّى، فَاجْعَلْنِي مِمَّنْ إِذَا سَأَلَكَ الْجَنَّةَ أَعْطَيْتَهُ، وَإِذَا تَعَوَّذَ بِكَ مِنَ النَّارِ أَعَذْتَهُ
duas.transliteration: Allāhumma inna hādhihi Muzdalifatu jumi'at fīhā alsina shattā tas'aluka ḥawā'ija shattā, faj'alnī mimman idhā sa'alaka al-jannata a'ṭaytahu, wa idhā ta'awwadha bika minan-nāri a'adhtahu.
duas.translation: O Allah, in Muzdalifah many tongues are gathered, each asking You for different needs. Make me among those whom when they ask You for Paradise, You grant it, and when they seek refuge with You from the Fire, You protect them.
duas.when_to_recite: Upon arriving at Muzdalifah
duas.source: From the supplications of the pious predecessors
duas.notes: Pray Maghrib and Isha combined and shortened upon arrival.

duas.title: Du'a when Throwing Each Pebble
duas.arabic: اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ
duas.transliteration: Allāhu akbar.
duas.translation: Allah is the Greatest.
duas.when_to_recite: With each of the 7 pebbles thrown at the Jamarat
duas.notes: After stoning the Jamarat al-Sughra and al-Wusta, face the Qiblah and raise your hands in du'a. No du'a after the Aqabah.

duas.title: Du'a for Animal Sacrifice
duas.arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُمَّ هَذَا مِنْكَ وَلَكَ، اللَّهُمَّ تَقَبَّلْ مِنِّي
duas.transliteration: Bismillāhi wallāhu akbar. Allāhumma hādhā minka wa lak. Allāhumma taqabbal minnī.
duas.translation: In the name of Allah, and Allah is the Greatest. O Allah, this is from You and for You. O Allah, accept it from me.
duas.when_to_recite: When performing or witnessing the animal sacrifice
duas.notes: If a proxy is performing the sacrifice, they recite this on your behalf.

duas.title: Du'a for Farewell Tawaf
duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ الْبَيْتُ بَيْتُكَ وَالْعَبْدُ عَبْدُكَ وَابْنُ عَبْدِكَ وَابْنُ أَمَتِكَ، حَمَلْتَنِي عَلَى مَا سَخَّرْتَ لِي مِنْ خَلْقِكَ حَتَّى سَيَّرْتَنِي فِي بِلَادِكَ وَبَلَّغْتَنِي بِنِعْمَتِكَ حَتَّى أَعَنْتَنِي عَلَى قَضَاءِ مَنَاسِكِكَ فَإِنْ كُنْتَ رَضِيتَ عَنِّي فَازْدَدْ عَنِّي رِضًا، وَإِلَّا فَمِنَ الْآنَ قَبْلَ أَنْ تَنْأَى عَنْ بَيْتِكَ دَارِي
duas.transliteration: Allāhumma al-baytu baytuka wal-'abdu 'abduka wabnu 'abdika wabnu amatik. Ḥamaltanī 'alā mā sakhkharta lī min khalqika ḥattā sayyartanī fī bilādika wa ballaghtanī bini'matika ḥattā a'antanī 'alā qaḍā'i manāsikik. Fa in kunta raḍīta 'annī faz'dad 'annī riḍan, wa illā faminal-āna qabla an tan'ā 'an baytika dārī.
duas.translation: O Allah, this House is Your House, and I am Your servant, son of Your servant, son of Your female servant. You carried me on what You have placed in service for me of Your creation, until You made me travel through Your lands and conveyed me through Your blessing until You helped me complete the rituals. If You are pleased with me, increase Your pleasure in me; otherwise, from now, before my home grows distant from Your House.
duas.when_to_recite: During the Farewell Tawaf
duas.notes: This is a deeply moving du'a. Many pilgrims weep during the Farewell Tawaf.

duas.title: Du'a when Drinking Zamzam
duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ عِلْمًا نَافِعًا وَرِزْقًا وَاسِعًا وَشِفَاءً مِنْ كُلِّ دَاءٍ
duas.transliteration: Allāhumma innī as'aluka 'ilman nāfi'an wa rizqan wāsi'an wa shifā'an min kulli dā'.
duas.translation: O Allah, I ask You for beneficial knowledge, abundant provision, and cure from every illness.
duas.when_to_recite: When drinking Zamzam water
duas.notes: Drink Zamzam while standing, facing the Qiblah, with three breaths.

duas.title: Du'a Entering the Masjid
duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ افْتَحْ لِي أَبْوَابَ رَحْمَتِكَ
duas.transliteration: Allāhumma iftaḥ lī abwāba raḥmatik.
duas.translation: O Allah, open for me the gates of Your mercy.
duas.when_to_recite: Upon entering any masjid, especially Masjid al-Haram
duas.notes: Enter with your right foot first.

duas.title: Du'a Leaving the Masjid
duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ مِنْ فَضْلِكَ
duas.transliteration: Allāhumma innī as'aluka min faḍlik.
duas.translation: O Allah, I ask You for Your bounty.
duas.when_to_recite: Upon leaving any masjid
duas.notes: Exit with your left foot first.

duas.title: Du'a upon First Seeing the Kaaba
duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ زِدْ هَذَا الْبَيْتَ تَشْرِيفًا وَتَعْظِيمًا وَتَكْرِيمًا وَمَهَابَةً، وَزِدْ مَنْ شَرَّفَهُ وَعَظَّمَهُ مِمَّنْ حَجَّهُ أَوِ اعْتَمَرَهُ تَشْرِيفًا وَتَكْرِيمًا وَتَعْظِيمًا وَبِرًّا
duas.transliteration: Allāhumma zid hādhal-bayta tashrīfan wa ta'ẓīman wa takrīman wa mahābah. Wa zid man sharrafahu wa 'aẓẓamahu mimman ḥajjahu awi'tamarahu tashrīfan wa takrīman wa ta'ẓīman wa birrā.
duas.translation: O Allah, increase this House in honor, reverence, dignity, and awe. And increase in honor, dignity, reverence, and piety those who honor and revere it among those who perform Hajj or Umrah.
duas.when_to_recite: Upon first beholding the Kaaba
duas.notes: The sight of the Kaaba is a moment of answered prayers. Raise your hands and make personal du'a.

duas.title: Salawat Ibrahim (Durood Ibrahim)
duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَى مُحَمَّدٍ وَعَلَى آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ كَمَا صَلَّيْتَ عَلَى إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَعَلَى آلِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ إِنَّكَ حَمِيدٌ مَجِيدٌ، اللَّهُمَّ بَارِكْ عَلَى مُحَمَّدٍ وَعَلَى آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ كَمَا بَارَكْتَ عَلَى إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَعَلَى آلِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ إِنَّكَ حَمِيدٌ مَجِيدٌ
duas.transliteration: Allāhumma ṣalli 'alā Muḥammadin wa 'alā āli Muḥammadin kamā ṣallayta 'alā Ibrāhīma wa 'alā āli Ibrāhīma innaka Ḥamīdun Majīd. Allāhumma bārik 'alā Muḥammadin wa 'alā āli Muḥammadin kamā bārakta 'alā Ibrāhīma wa 'alā āli Ibrāhīma innaka Ḥamīdun Majīd.
duas.translation: O Allah, send blessings upon Muhammad and upon the family of Muhammad, as You sent blessings upon Ibrahim and upon the family of Ibrahim. You are indeed Praiseworthy, Glorious. O Allah, send blessings upon Muhammad and upon the family of Muhammad, as You blessed Ibrahim and the family of Ibrahim. You are indeed Praiseworthy, Glorious.
duas.when_to_recite: In prayer and throughout Hajj, especially near Maqam Ibrahim
duas.notes: Sending salawat on the Prophet ﷺ is highly virtuous throughout Hajj.

duas.title: Istighfar — Seeking Forgiveness
duas.arabic: أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ الْعَظِيمَ الَّذِي لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ وَأَتُوبُ إِلَيْهِ
duas.transliteration: Astaghfirullāhal-'aẓīmallladhī lā ilāha illā huwal-Ḥayyul-Qayyūmu wa atūbu ilayh.
duas.translation: I seek forgiveness from Allah, the Mighty, other than whom there is no God, the Living, the Sustaining, and I repent to Him.
duas.when_to_recite: Abundantly throughout Hajj, especially at Arafat and in Mina
duas.notes: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever says this, Allah will forgive him even if he fled from battle.'

duas.title: Subhanallah, Alhamdulillah, Allahu Akbar
duas.arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ وَلَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ
duas.transliteration: Subḥānallāhi walḥamdu lillāhi wa lā ilāha illallāhu wallāhu akbar.
duas.translation: Glory be to Allah, all praise is for Allah, there is no god but Allah, and Allah is the Greatest.
duas.when_to_recite: Constantly throughout Hajj — walking, waiting, resting
duas.notes: The Prophet ﷺ said these four words are the most beloved speech to Allah.
